title: Golden Hour Hike at Rock Canyon
description: Time your hike to reach the upper switchbacks of Rock Canyon right as the sun dips behind the western mountains. The canyon walls glow with warm light, making it one of the most romantic short hikes in Provo.
proTip: Start about 90 minutes before sunset to hit the sweet spot where the light filters through the canyon walls.

title: Sunrise Summit at Y Mountain
description: Wake up early and hike to the iconic Y on the mountainside above BYU campus as the sun rises over the Wasatch Range. The panoramic views of Utah Valley bathed in morning light are absolutely breathtaking.
proTip: Bring headlamps and a blanket to sit on at the Y while you wait for the sunrise. Hot cocoa in a thermos earns bonus points.

title: Picnic by Bridal Veil Falls
description: Spread a blanket near one of Utah's most photogenic waterfalls and enjoy a casual picnic with the mist cooling you off. The double-cataract waterfall is easily accessible right off the Provo Canyon road.
proTip: Go on a weekday to avoid crowds. Pack sandwiches and fruit so you can keep things simple and focus on the scenery.

title: Stargazing at Squaw Peak Overlook
description: Drive up the Squaw Peak Road to the overlook and lay out blankets for a stunning stargazing session high above the valley lights. On clear nights you can see the Milky Way arching across the sky.
proTip: Download a stargazing app like SkyView before you go. Bring warm layers even in summer—it gets chilly at elevation.

title: Couples Bike Ride on the Provo River Trail
description: Rent bikes and pedal together along the scenic Provo River Parkway, a paved trail that winds through tree-lined corridors and alongside the river. The 15-mile trail offers a flat, easy ride perfect for conversation and enjoying nature.
proTip: Start near the Provo Recreation Center and ride north toward the canyon for the prettiest stretch along the river.

title: Underground Adventure at Timpanogos Cave
description: Hike 1.5 miles up to the cave entrance and then explore stunning underground formations with stalactites, stalagmites, and the famous heart-shaped formation. It's a unique date that combines a moderate hike with a guided cave experience.
address: 2038 Alpine Loop Rd, American Fork, UT 84003
proTip: Reserve tickets online well in advance—they sell out fast in summer. Wear sturdy shoes for the steep paved trail.

title: Sunset Watch at Utah Lake State Park
description: Drive out to Utah Lake State Park and find a spot along the shore to watch the sun set over the lake with the distant mountains silhouetted against the sky. The wide-open water creates stunning reflections during golden hour.
proTip: Bring camp chairs and a small speaker with soft music. The west-facing shore gives you an unobstructed sunset view.

title: Scenic Drive Through Hobble Creek Canyon
description: Take a leisurely drive through Hobble Creek Canyon with the windows down, winding through maple and oak groves that explode with color in autumn. Pull off at picnic areas along the way to stretch your legs and soak in the scenery.
address: Hobble Creek Canyon Rd, Springville, UT 84663
proTip: Peak fall colors usually hit in early to mid-October. Make a playlist for the drive to set the mood.

title: Night Hike on Maple Mountain
description: Grab headlamps and hike the trails on Maple Mountain after dark for a thrilling nighttime adventure with views of Springville and the valley lights below. The quiet of the mountain at night makes every sound more vivid.
proTip: Stick to trails you've hiked in daylight first. Bring extra batteries for your headlamp and tell someone your route.

title: Fly Fishing Date on the Provo River
description: Try your hand at fly fishing together on the Lower Provo River, one of Utah's premier trout streams. Whether you're experienced or total beginners, casting a line in the canyon is peaceful and surprisingly fun as a couple.
proTip: You'll each need a Utah fishing license—buy them online for about $8 each. Bring waders if you have them or fish from the bank.

title: Fall Colors on the Alpine Loop
description: Drive the Alpine Loop Scenic Byway through American Fork Canyon and around Mount Timpanogos for one of the most spectacular fall foliage drives in the West. Stop at overlooks for photos with blazing reds, oranges, and golds.
address: Alpine Loop Rd, American Fork, UT 84003
proTip: There's a $6 day-use fee. Go mid-week in late September or early October for peak color without the weekend traffic.

title: Stargazing from Rock Canyon Park
description: Bring a telescope or binoculars to Rock Canyon Park after dark and explore the night sky from this easily accessible spot at the canyon mouth. The mountains block some light pollution, giving you cleaner views of constellations.
proTip: Check the moon phase—a new moon night gives the darkest sky. The BYU astronomy club sometimes sets up telescopes here.

title: Kayaking on Utah Lake
description: Rent a tandem kayak and paddle out onto Utah Lake, exploring the shoreline and enjoying the wide-open water with mountain views in every direction. It's a peaceful way to spend a morning together on the water.
proTip: Go in the morning when the lake is calm. Afternoons can get windy and choppy. Wear sunscreen and bring water.

title: Stroll the Bonneville Shoreline Trail
description: Walk hand-in-hand along the Bonneville Shoreline Trail above Provo, which traces the ancient shoreline of Lake Bonneville along the mountainside. The gentle elevation and stunning valley views make it ideal for long conversation.
address: Near 2230 N Temple View Dr, Provo, UT 84604
proTip: The section near the Provo Temple gives some of the best valley views. It's mostly flat and well-maintained.

title: Moonlit Walk in Provo Canyon
description: On a full moon night, walk along the paved Provo River Parkway through the canyon with the moonlight reflecting off the river. The sound of rushing water and the silvery light create an unforgettable romantic atmosphere.
proTip: Check a lunar calendar and plan for a full moon. Start at Vivian Park and walk south—the paved trail is easy to follow in moonlight.

title: Hidden Waterfall Hike at Grove Creek
description: Hike the short but rewarding Grove Creek Trail in Pleasant Grove to reach a tucked-away waterfall that most people don't know about. The trail winds through scrub oak and offers beautiful canyon views.
address: Grove Creek Trailhead, Pleasant Grove, UT 84062
proTip: The trail is only about 2 miles round trip but has some scrambling near the waterfall. Bring shoes with good grip.

title: Overnight Camping at South Fork Park
description: Set up camp at South Fork Park in Hobble Creek Canyon for a cozy overnight in the mountains. Fall asleep under the stars beside a crackling campfire and wake up to the sound of the creek.
address: Hobble Creek Canyon Rd, Springville, UT 84663
proTip: Bring firewood from town—collecting wood in the canyon is restricted. Reserve your campsite online ahead of time.

title: Night Drive Up Squaw Peak Road
description: Drive the winding Squaw Peak Road after dark and stop at pulloffs to take in the sparkling lights of Utah Valley spread out far below. It's an easy and free way to feel like you've escaped the world together.
proTip: The road closes in winter. Go on a weeknight for fewer cars and more privacy at the pulloffs.

title: Morning Hike Through Dry Canyon
description: Start your morning with a hike through Dry Canyon in Lindon, a quiet trail with wildflowers in spring and stunning red-rock-like terrain. The canyon feels surprisingly remote despite being close to neighborhoods.
proTip: Go early to beat the heat in summer. Bring plenty of water—the canyon is exposed and can get hot.

title: Bonfire on the Shores of Utah Lake
description: Build a small bonfire on the shores of Utah Lake as the sun goes down and enjoy the warmth together under the open sky. Roast marshmallows and watch the stars come out over the water.
proTip: Check local fire regulations before going—sometimes restrictions are in place. Bring a fire pan or use an existing fire ring.

title: Guided Fly Fishing Lesson for Two
description: Book a guided fly fishing lesson for two on the Provo River and learn to cast together with expert instruction. The Middle Provo River is world-class trout water, and a guide makes it accessible for complete beginners.
proTip: Book through a local outfitter like Rocky Mountain Outfitters. Most guides provide all gear—just bring sunscreen and snacks.

title: Nature Walk at Cascade Springs
description: Take the gentle boardwalk trail at Cascade Springs, where natural springs bubble up and flow through lush pools surrounded by wildflowers and aspen groves. It's one of the most serene and beautiful spots near Provo.
address: Cascade Springs Dr, Wasatch-Cache National Forest, UT
proTip: Accessible via the Alpine Loop ($6 fee). The boardwalk is wheelchair-accessible and great for a relaxed pace.

title: Outdoor Bouldering at Rock Canyon
description: Rent a crash pad from a local gear shop and try outdoor bouldering together on the beginner-friendly rocks near the mouth of Rock Canyon. It's a fun physical challenge that requires teamwork and trust—plus the canyon setting is gorgeous.
proTip: Bring a crash pad if you have one, or start on low traverse problems. The boulders on the south side of the trail are the most beginner-friendly.

title: Hike to Tibble Fork Reservoir
description: Drive up American Fork Canyon and hike around the turquoise-blue Tibble Fork Reservoir surrounded by towering mountains. The short loop trail around the reservoir is flat and easy, perfect for talking and enjoying the scenery.
address: Tibble Fork Rd, American Fork, UT 84003
proTip: There's a $6 canyon fee. Visit in early morning for mirror-like reflections on the water and fewer crowds.

title: Creekside Picnic in Hobble Creek Canyon
description: Find a quiet picnic spot along the creek in Hobble Creek Canyon and enjoy lunch with the sound of running water and birds singing in the maples. Several pulloffs have picnic tables right next to the water.
address: Hobble Creek Canyon Rd, Springville, UT 84663
proTip: The Cherry Campground area has some of the best creekside spots. Bring a blanket to sit on the rocks by the water.

title: Meteor Shower Watching from Provo Peak
description: During a meteor shower, drive up toward Provo Peak and find a dark spot away from city lights to watch shooting stars streak across the sky. Utah's dry, clear air makes this one of the best places in the country for meteor viewing.
proTip: The Perseid shower in August and Geminid shower in December are the best. Arrive at least 30 minutes early so your eyes adjust to the dark.

title: Wildflower Hike on Mount Timpanogos
description: Hike the Timpooneke or Aspen Grove trail on Mount Timpanogos during peak wildflower season when the alpine meadows are carpeted in columbine, Indian paintbrush, and bluebells. The views of the glacial cirque are jaw-dropping.
address: Alpine Loop Rd, American Fork, UT 84003
proTip: You don't have to summit—hike as far as the meadows (about 3-4 miles) for the best wildflowers. Peak bloom is usually mid-July.

title: Scenic Ski Lift Ride at Sundance Resort
description: Ride the ski lift at Sundance Mountain Resort during summer or fall for sweeping views of Mount Timpanogos and the surrounding canyons. At the top, hike the Ridge Trail or simply sit and take in the panoramic mountain scenery.
address: 8841 N Alpine Loop Rd, Sundance, UT 84604
proTip: Go in late September for fall colors from the lift. Pair it with dinner at the Foundry Grill to make it a full date.

title: Horseback Riding Through Provo Canyon
description: Book a guided horseback ride through Provo Canyon and explore trails you can't reach on foot while the guide shares local history and nature lore. Riding side by side through the canyon is a classic Western romantic experience.
proTip: Book with a local ranch and ask for a sunset ride if available. Wear closed-toe shoes and long pants.

title: Wading and Skipping Stones on the Upper Provo River
description: Drive into upper Provo Canyon past Vivian Park and find a shallow stretch of the Provo River to wade in and skip stones together. The cold mountain water and smooth river rocks make for a refreshing and playful afternoon.
specificLocation: Upper Provo River near Vivian Park
proTip: Bring water shoes or old sneakers—the river rocks are slippery. A change of socks is a game-changer for the drive home.
